pages.title: COVID Updates and Safety - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: COVID Updates and Safety
Your safety is our number one priority so we have invested in additional personal protective equipment that goes beyond the CDC standard for safety.
Our 3 Layers of Protection are: HEPA Air Filters with UV Sterilization…Extra Oral Suction System…and Organic Hypochlorous Acid Cleaning.

As our guest, you will notice that in every room we have installed new HEPA Air Purifiers with UV sterilization. The HEPA filter captures allergens and the UV light goes beyond filtration to destroy viruses and bacteria in the air.
During all dental procedures, extra-oral suction systems will also be used to remove, what we call, the bio cloud of aerosol droplets from around our guests during their visit.

Lastly, we use a fogger to spray hypochlorous acid on all surfaces during our sanitation process. Hypochlorous acid is an EPA-registered organic disinfectant used to eliminate viruses and bacteria from flat surfaces and the air. This system has been proven effective at killing COVID-19 and other viruses.

In addition to these 3 methods, you will be offered hand sanitizer at the front desk and can use it at the beginning and end of your visit. We sanitize each room in between each guest and wipe down and sanitize all pens and surfaces in the front office for your safety.
We are accepting a limited amount of patients Monday-Saturday at our regular business hours. Please call to schedule your next visit.
Monday – 7:00AM – 7:00PM Tuesday – 7:00AM – 7:00PM Wednesday – 7:00AM – 7:00PM Thursday – 10:00AM – 7:00PM Friday – 8:00AM – 1:00PM Saturday – 8:00AM – 2:00PM Sunday – Closed

Please arrive with a face mask and expect to have your temperature taken prior to going back to see the dentist. We ask that you practice social distancing while in our office to protect yourself and other guests.
We ask that you sign your forms electronically that we send via text. If you difficulty, we created a short how-to video to assist you.
If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to give us a call and ask!
2900 Bristol st. A205 Costa Mesa, CA 92626
Monday – Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM Friday – Sunday: Closed

headings.text: Meet 5-Star Rated Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist, Dr. Mark Nguyen Of OC Healthy Smiles
Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist – Mark Nguyen, DDS

paragraphs: As a top-rated Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, dentistry is Dr. Nguyen’s passion

A native of Orange County, CA, Dr. Mark K. Nguyen earned his Doctorate of Dental Surgery at the world-renowned New York University College of Dentistry and is licensed to practice in both California and Nevada. As a top-rated Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, dentistry is his passion.

After a general practice residency at the Loma Linda VA Hospital, Invisalign certification, and a clinical concentration program in cosmetic dentistry, Dr. Nguyen is uniquely qualified to create beautiful and healthy smiles. Dr. Nguyen’s true passion for Dentistry is exemplified by his participation in continuing education that far exceeds requirements to maintain a dental license.
Dr. Nguyen is a past and current member of the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry, Academy of General Dentistry, American Academy of Implant Dentistry, and the California Dental Association.

When not in his Costa Mesa dental office, Dr. Nguyen enjoys spending time with his family and friends. Dr. Nguyen and his wife, Lindsay, are avid outdoor enthusiasts and usually spend their Sunday mornings surfing or biking at the beach. As a way of giving back to the community, Dr. Nguyen provides a Dentistry From the Heart Day, in which free dentistry is donated to those in the community who lack access to dental care.
2900 Bristol st. A205 Costa Mesa, CA 92626
Monday – Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM Friday – Sunday: Closed

headings.text: Meet Orange County, CA Endodontist, Dr. Joelle Tavitian of OC Healthy Smiles
Orange County, CA, Endodontist – Dr. Joelle Tavitian, DDS

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office
Dr. Joelle Tavitian is a personable, highly enthusiastic Orange County, CA, endodontist, with state of the art training in modern endodontic dentistry.

Dr. Joelle Tavitian received her dental degree in 1999 from the University of Southern California. She then pursued two additional years of education after receiving her dental degree. She completed her endodontic specialty training in 2001 from the University of Southern California, with knowledge and experience in all aspects of morphology, physiology, and pathology of the human dental pulp and periradicular tissues.

Dr. Tavitian understands that dentistry has many built-in anxieties, and she is committed to providing excellence in Endodontic services in a professional, caring, and comfortable atmosphere, with exceptional and unmatched customer service.

When not in her Costa Mesa dental office, Dr. Tavitian enjoys traveling, live concerts, and theater with family and friends. She sometimes mixes her passion for travel with volunteer work abroad by providing free dental care for orphaned children and less privileged communities in Armenia.

pages.title: Dr. Austin Burnett - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Dr. Burnett was born and raised in Southern California. He was an Advanced Placement/International Baccalaureate student at Troy High School in Fullerton where he graduated from the Troy Tech program with High Honors and as a member of the National Honor Society and California Scholarship Federation.

After 15 years in Orange County, Dr. Burnett left California with an academic scholarship to pursue his undergraduate degree at Brigham Young University in Utah. There he obtained his Bachelor of Science degree in Microbiology and minors in Chemistry and Japanese. Dr. Burnett graduated Magna cum laude from the University of Nevada, Las Vegas School of Dental Medicine.

While in dental school he served as the Clinic Director of a volunteer clinic donating free dental care to military veterans (Sgt. Clint Ferrin Memorial Veterans Dental Clinic) and was the president of the Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery club. He also served as a Pre-Dental Student Coordinator for the American Student Dental Association and as an Assistant Course Instructor at the dental school.

Dr. Burnett published multiple times while in dental school including an abstract on stem cell use in dentistry, a research project for which he was awarded a competitive research grant. Dr. Burnett completed his Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery training at Cook County Hospital, a level one trauma center in Chicago (see ER the tv show). He was awarded the Resident of the Year award twice while in residency, served as an adjunct faculty member for the Midwestern University College of Dental Medicine, and presented at the Chicago Society of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons biannual meeting on malignant transformation of odontogenic keratocysts.

Dr. Burnett currently works both in private practice and as a Clinical Educator at the University of California, Los Angeles School of Dentistry (UCLA). He has completed certifications in Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support (ACLS), Pediatric Advanced Life Support (PALS), Advanced Trauma Life Support (ATLS), and Basic Life Support (BLS). He has active hospital privileges at Orange County Global Medical Center in Santa Ana. Dr. Burnett is a member of the American Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons (AAOMS), the California Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons (CALAOMS), the American Dental Society of Anesthesiology (ADSA), the California Dental Society of Anesthesiology (CDSA), the American Academy of Implant Dentistry (AAID), the American Dental Association (ADA), the California Dental Association (CDA), and the Orange County Dental Society (OCDS).

Dr. Burnett married his wife Elise in Redlands, CA. They currently have three children: Caden (8), Vance (6), and Macey Jane (2). Dr. Burnett is an Eagle Scout, black belt in Tae Kwon Do, avid guitarist, passionate soccer player, and die-hard Lakers fan!

paragraphs: Dr. Gabriella Chung (Dr. Gaby) was born in Indonesia and raised in Culver City, California. She earned her B.S. in Biological Sciences from the University of California, Santa Barbara, where she discovered her passion for dentistry through volunteer experiences in Mexico and Nicaragua. Witnessing firsthand the profound impact dental care can have on individuals and communities inspired her to pursue a career dedicated to improving patients’ quality of life.

Dr. Gaby graduated with honors from Midwestern University in Downers Grove, Illinois, earning her Doctor of Dental Medicine degree. She is passionate about all aspects of dentistry and is committed to staying current with the latest techniques and evidence-based practices to provide the highest standard of care for her patients.
Outside of the office, Dr. Gaby enjoys strength training, playing tennis, traveling, and exploring new restaurants

pages.title: Top Provider of Invisalign in Orange County, CA - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Top Provider of Invisalign in Orange County, CA
What Are The Benefits Of Invisalign?
Simulate Your Smile With iTero Technology
Fast-Track Your Invisalign Treatment With Propel

paragraphs: Invisalign – The Clear & Comfortable Way To Straighten Your Teeth

At our all-in-one dental practice, we are thrilled to be the top provider of Invisalign among dentists in Orange County, CA. Our Invisalign-certified dental office offers Invisalign to patients who want to straighten their teeth without friends, family, or coworkers noticing.

Invisalign straightens your teeth in much the same way braces would, except its state-of-the-art technology means you won’t have any brackets semi-permanently glued to your teeth. Invisalign uses removable, clear plastic aligners that are custom made for your unique smile. These smooth, comfortable, and virtually invisible trays fit snugly over your teeth and gradually shift them into their proper alignment, according to a precise path of movement designed by your OC Healthy Smiles dentist and Invisalign’s revolutionary computer software. Every two weeks, you will replace your aligner with a new one that represents the next step in your teeth’s movement. That’s all there is to it!
With Invisalign, you can enjoy a whole host of benefits over traditional metal braces, including:
See a 3D simulation of your smile before and after Invisalign treatment at your FREE consultation.

The iTero scan will create an interactive 3D model of your smile that will allow you to see how your smile can be improved with Invisalign. This new technology will eliminate the need for messy and sometimes gooey impressions. The iTero will not only assist you in visualizing your new smile, but it will allow your OC Healthy Smiles dentist to more accurately prescribe treatment.

While you straighten your teeth with Invisalign, our Invisalign dentists in Orange County, CA, can also shorten your overall treatment time thanks to the acceleration provided by Propel. Propel’s innovative devices and procedures allow your teeth to shift much more quickly than with Invisalign alone – in some cases, up to 50% faster! To learn more about how Propel fast-tracks Invisalign aligner therapy, call us and schedule your appointment at OC Healthy Smiles today!

pages.title: Community Outreach: Giving Back to the Orange County Community - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Community Outreach: Giving Back to the Orange County Community
Community Outreach: Giving Back To The Orange County Community
Cali Taco & Project Hope Alliance Collaboration
Giving Back To Our Community One Patient At A Time
Providing Dental Care For Local Children

paragraphs: Join In And Support Our Community!

In 2022, OC Healthy Smiles proudly provided free cleanings, extractions, and fillings for over 25 veterans and active service members as part of our Veterans Day event. We look forward to expanding our efforts next year and making an even greater impact in the Veteran community.

We are excited to announce our 5th annual FREE Dentistry Day, Smiles for Veterans. On November 5th from 8AM-1PM, our dental team will be providing free cleanings, extractions, or fillings for active service members and veterans. Walk-ins are welcome, but we encourage those interested to call today and reserve a time. Availability is VERY limited, so call NOW.

We look forward to serving this underserved community once again and DOUBLING our impact on the community from last year’s event. Please help us reach more veterans by sharing this post with a veteran in your life and on your social media pages.
Call (714)545-0484 to get scheduled for your free extraction, filling, or cleaning.

Thanks to your help, OC Healthy Smiles and Cali Tacos donated over $1000 to Project Hope Alliance. Project Hope Alliance works with students to help end the cycle of homelessness. Should we do this again with Cali Tacos or another local restaurant? Let us know! Learn more about Project Hope Alliance and how they are supporting local homeless students HERE

We are so proud to have donated over $3,000 worth of free dentistry to locals who were in-need of dental care last week. We are so fortunate to be able to open up our practice twice a year to individuals in-need and help our community achieve better oral health. Next time, we hope to double our donated dentistry. We can’t wait to do this again in November!

OC Healthy Smiles is dedicated to helping all of their guests and the local community get to their best smile. For Veterans Day, the OC Healthy Smiles team provided free dental services for veterans and armed service members as a thank you for their service.

We are happy to announce the winner of our 10K Smile Makeover is Tara! Back at the beginning of the year, we asked for you to nominate someone who you felt deserved a smile makeover and Tara’s compelling story prompted us to crown her as our winner and start her smile makeover. Tara is an amazing woman who not only beat cancer but is using it to fuel her passion to help others. She is currently in medical school to become a doctor to help others fight hard in their battle with cancer. You can watch the beginning of Tara’s story and follow along with her smile makeover on our Instagram @OC_Healthy_Smiles.

On November 8th, 2019, OC Healthy Smiles provided free cleanings, extractions, or fillings for over 20 veterans and active service members. We continue to celebrate Veterans Month by donating $100 to the Wounded Warriors Project for each newly referred patient this month. We are excited to continue this next year and help even more of the Veteran community.
On March 15th, 2019 our dental team provided FREE dentistry to 15 members of the local community making it our most successful free dentistry day to date!
If you are interested in attending one of our free dentistry days, follow our social media and bookmark this page for updates! We look forward to serving you and your friends in the future.

Unfortunately, most Veterans don’t qualify for VA Dental care, so OC Healthy Smiles provided free dentistry to local Veterans on November 16th, 2018. At this event, 14 veterans received dental care, and many of whom have not been to the dentist in over 10 years. It was an honor to serve this underserved community and we are excited to do this again next year!
Thanks to all of you, we were able to drop off 50 jars of PB&J to Project Hope Alliances food bank! They will be distributing these jars to local families in need during the holiday season.
Why PB&J? A PB&J sandwich is a good source of protein, healthy fats, fiber, and B vitamins!

On March 15th, 2018 the OC Healthy Smiles staff provided free services for the local community. Over $5,000 worth of treatment was donated to the attendees who normally would not have access to dental care.
Part of the OC Healthy Smiles mission is to improve the community lives through quality care, compassion, and education.
Thank you to Project Hope Alliance for partnering with us for this event to help us reach more of the local community!

Over $2,500 in dentistry was donated to U.S Veterans and armed service members in November of 2017 at out 1st Annual Smiles for Veterans. In honor of Veterans Day, OC Healthy Smiles provided free dentistry for this underserved community. Dr. Mark Nguyen did his residency training at the Department of Veterans Affairs and saw firsthand how difficult it is for veterans to access dental care.
From all of us here at OC Healthy Smiles, we would like to thank all service members and veterans for their service. We look forward to serving more veterans next year when we host this event again.

Giving back to the Orange County community is one of our core beliefs at OC Healthy Smiles. That is why we have partnered with Dentistry from the Heart and Project Hope Alliance to provide free dental care to those in need. Our goal is to help make the Orange County area a better place to live.
Here is a video of one our Dentistry from the Heart patients, Brett, who bravely served our country in the Marines. Helping him was such an amazing experience.

Below are pictures of the OC Healthy Smiles team providing free dental care to homeless children through the Project Hope Alliance. There are more than 26,000 homeless children attending Orange County Schools. We were happy to improve the smiles of many of these children.
2900 Bristol st. A205 Costa Mesa, CA 92626
Monday – Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM Friday – Sunday: Closed

For Veterans Day, the OC Healthy Smiles team provided free dental services for veterans and armed service members as a thank you for their service. $10,000 Smile Giveaway We are happy to announce the winner of our 10K Smile Makeover is Tara! Back at the beginning of the year, we asked for you to nominate someone who you felt deserved a smile makeover and Tara’s compelling story prompted us to crown her as our winner and start her smile makeover. Tara is an amazing woman who not only beat cancer but is using it to fuel her passion to help others. She is currently in medical school to become a doctor to help others fight hard in their battle with cancer. You can watch the beginning of Tara’s story and follow along with her smile makeover on our Instagram @OC_Healthy_Smiles. 3rd Annual Smiles for Veterans On November 8th, 2019, OC Healthy Smiles provided free cleanings, extractions, or fillings for over 20 veterans and active service members. We continue to celebrate Veterans Month by donating $100 to the Wounded Warriors Project for each newly referred patient this month. We are excited to continue this next year and help even more of the Veteran community. Smiles for Costa Mesa 2019 On March 15th, 2019 our dental team provided FREE dentistry to 15 members of the local community making it our most successful free dentistry day to date! If you are interested in attending one of our free dentistry days, follow our social media and bookmark this page for updates! We look forward to serving you and your friends in the future. 2nd Annual Smiles for Veterans Unfortunately, most Veterans don’t qualify for VA Dental care, so OC Healthy Smiles provided free dentistry to local Veterans on November 16th, 2018. At this event, 14 veterans received dental care, and many of whom have not been to the dentist in over 10 years. It was an honor to serve this underserved community and we are excited to do this again next year! PB&J Drive Thanks to all of you, we were able to drop off 50 jars of PB&J to Project Hope Alliances food bank! They will be distributing these jars to local families in need during the holiday season. Why PB&J? A PB&J sandwich is a good source of protein, healthy fats, fiber, and B vitamins! Smiles for Costa Mesa 2018 On March 15th, 2018 the OC Healthy Smiles staff provided free services for the local community. Over $5,000 worth of treatment was donated to the attendees who normally would not have access to dental care. Part of the OC Healthy Smiles mission is to improve the community lives through quality care, compassion, and education. Thank you to Project Hope Alliance for partnering with us for this event to help us reach more of the local community! 1st Annual Smiles For Veterans Over $2,500 in dentistry was donated to U.S Veterans and armed service members in November of 2017 at out 1st Annual Smiles for Veterans. In honor of Veterans Day, OC Healthy Smiles provided free dentistry for this underserved community. Dr. Mark Nguyen did his residency training at the Department of Veterans Affairs and saw firsthand how difficult it is for veterans to access dental care. From all of us here at OC Healthy Smiles, we would like to thank all service members and veterans for their service. We look forward to serving more veterans next year when we host this event again. Giving Back To Our Community One Patient At A Time Giving back to the Orange County community is one of our core beliefs at OC Healthy Smiles. That is why we have partnered with Dentistry from the Heart and Project Hope Alliance to provide free dental care to those in need. Our goal is to help make the Orange County area a better place to live. Here is a video of one our Dentistry from the Heart patients, Brett, who bravely served our country in the Marines. Helping him was such an amazing experience. Providing Dental Care For Local Children Below are pictures of the OC Healthy Smiles team providing free dental care to homeless children through the Project Hope Alliance. There are more than 26,000 homeless children attending Orange County Schools. We were happy to improve the smiles of many of these children. Oc Healthy Smiles 2900 Bristol st. A proud mom of a young son, she enjoys traveling with her family, exploring new cuisines and experiencingdiHerent cultures. headings.text: MEET THE ORANGE COUNTY DENTAL TEAM OF OC HEALTHY SMILES

paragraphs: Tish has been in the dental field since 2006. She graduated from Hofstra University with a degree in Business Administration. Her experience in patient care, insurance, and customer service is an asset to our office. Her passion for helping patients shows in her willingness to go above and beyond.
She lives in Huntington Beach. In her free time, she enjoys spending time with family and hiking with her dog, Reid.
Tish is available during office hours to help or answer any questions or concerns you may have. Consider her your personal concierge at OC Healthy Smiles.

Tawnya is our Scheduling Coordinator. She grew up in Orange County and has always been fascinated by dental health and how it has a direct correlation to our overall health. In her free time, she enjoys traveling, going to concerts, and spending time at Disneyland.

As an Orange County native, I grew up surrounded by bodies of water. After high school, I spent several years in Western Kentucky. I have a passion for all creative pursuits and enjoy reading. My academic background includes a bachelor’s degree in psychology with a focus on abnormal and deviant psychology. Transitioning into the dental field, I aimed to be a friendly and reassuring presence for guests who may experience dental anxiety.

Thais is a dedicated and compassionate dental hygienist who takes pride in helping patients feel comfortable and confident about their oral health. With a gentle touch and a genuine passion for education, she loves empowering patients with tips and tools to keep their smiles healthy between visits.

Outside of the office, Thais enjoys spending time with family and friends, exploring new restaurants, and staying active outdoors. Her caring nature and dedication make her an invaluable part of the OC Healthy Smiles team!
Mo is the friendly face you will see when entering the back office, she enjoys getting to know each patient and providing a warm, caring, and stress- free environment.
In her spare time, she loves spending time with her fur baby, Mowgli, and staying active.

Inspired by her stepmom, Hailey chose dentistry to enhance smiles and impact oral health positively. Beyond dentistry, she enjoys dance classes and video games with her boyfriend. She has a twin sister, four older sisters, a brother, and three dogs.

Bailey has always been passionate about working in healthcare and found her calling in dentistry, where she can combine her love for people with helping others feel healthy and confident about their smiles. She enjoys getting to know patients and being part of the transformation that helps them look and feel their best.

In the office, Bailey brings positivity, teamwork, and a calm presence to every appointment. Outside of work, she loves surfing, practicing Pilates, playing instruments, and writing music. Her creative spirit and caring heart make her a wonderful addition to the OC Healthy Smiles family.

Sharyah joined OC Healthy Smiles with a passion for helping people and a goal of becoming a Registered Dental Hygienist. She chose dental assisting as the perfect stepping stone to gain hands-on experience and learn from an incredible team while pursuing her dream.

She loves assisting with crown procedures and seeing patients leave with confident, restored smiles. Known for her caring nature and positive attitude, Sharyah makes every visit feel comfortable and welcoming.
When she’s not in the office, Sharyah enjoys going on walks, cooking new recipes, and spending quality time with her family.

Klaudine is our Lead RDA who brings a smile to every patient she meets! When she’s not making patients feel comfortable, you can find her at Disneyland making memories with her kids. She’s also a big fan of pandas and loves sharing her joy with everyone around her.
2900 Bristol st. A205 Costa Mesa, CA 92626

headings.text: 5-Star-Rated Dentists for Dental Cleanings in Costa Mesa, CA
5-star-rated Dentists For Dental Cleanings In Costa Mesa, Ca
State-Of-The-Art, Quality Dental Cleanings In Costa Mesa, CA

paragraphs: At OC Healthy Smiles, our mission is to provide the finest dental care, including quality dental cleanings in Costa Mesa, CA.

Brushing and flossing your teeth are key to maintaining a healthy smile, but they aren’t the only steps you should take to improve the strength and longevity of your teeth and gums. While brushing is effective at removing most of the plaque build-up on your teeth, it won’t remove the more stubborn tartar that can build up over time.

Tartar is a hardened deposit on your tooth that results from a buildup of plaque along the gums. These hardened deposits, if not routinely removed, can cause cavities, tooth decay, and gum disease. This is why regular dental cleanings are important. Routine or preventive cleaning appointments should be a part of everyone’s dental hygiene regimen at least once every six months, to maintain optimal oral health.

During your regular dental cleanings, our Orange County dentist will also provide you with a thorough oral exam to identify any other potential concerns that could affect your dental or overall health. Our dentist will check your teeth for any signs of decay or cavities, you gums for periodontal disease, and any restorative work you may have (such as dental veneers, dental crowns, and dental implants) for signs of wear and tear.

Our dentists in Orange County, CA, and dental team are here to provide you with a thoroughly enjoyable dental visit when you see us for your dental cleaning and exam. We love to offer state-of-the-art, quality dental care services with a smile to patients in and around Costa Mesa, Newport Beach, Newport Coast, Irvine, Fountain Valley, Corona del Mar, Huntington Beach, Tustin, Laguna Beach, Santa Ana, California (CA).
To schedule your dental cleaning with OC Healthy Smiles, call us at 714.422.0596 or click here to use our online appointment request form.

Reserve A Visit 5-star-rated Dentists For Dental Cleanings In Costa Mesa, Ca Brushing and flossing your teeth are key to maintaining a healthy smile, but they aren’t the only steps you should take to improve the strength and longevity of your teeth and gums. While brushing is effective at removing most of the plaque build-up on your teeth, it won’t remove the more stubborn tartar that can build up over time. Tartar is a hardened deposit on your tooth that results from a buildup of plaque along the gums. These hardened deposits, if not routinely removed, can cause cavities, tooth decay, and gum disease. This is why regular dental cleanings are important. Routine or preventive cleaning appointments should be a part of everyone’s dental hygiene regimen at least once every six months, to maintain optimal oral health. State-Of-The-Art, Quality Dental Cleanings In Costa Mesa, CA During your regular dental cleanings, our Orange County dentist will also provide you with a thorough oral exam to identify any other potential concerns that could affect your dental or overall health. Our dentist will check your teeth for any signs of decay or cavities, you gums for periodontal disease, and any restorative work you may have (such as dental veneers, dental crowns, and dental implants) for signs of wear and tear. Our dentists in Orange County, CA, and dental team are here to provide you with a thoroughly enjoyable dental visit when you see us for your dental cleaning and exam. We love to offer state-of-the-art, quality dental care services with a smile to patients in and around Costa Mesa, Newport Beach, Newport Coast, Irvine, Fountain Valley, Corona del Mar, Huntington Beach, Tustin, Laguna Beach, Santa Ana, California (CA). To schedule your dental cleaning with OC Healthy Smiles, call us at 714.422.0596 or click here to use our online appointment request form. pages.title: Dental Veneers - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Our Cosmetic Dentists In Costa Mesa, CA, Can Improve The Overall Look Of Your Teeth With Porcelain Veneers

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

If you are not comfortable with the shape, length, or overall look of your teeth, then dental veneers may be a great option for your smile. Porcelain veneers are thin layers that cover your existing teeth to provide the ideal gum-to-tooth ratio that produces an aesthetic smile. Veneers can also be used to cover stained teeth that won’t respond to teeth whitening treatment.

Our 5-star-rated cosmetic dentists in Costa Mesa, CA, are able to offer porcelain veneers as one of our advanced dentistry options. You can feel confident knowing that your perfect smile is only a few visits away when you choose OC Healthy Smiles for your veneers. pages.title: Dental Bridges - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

If you have a missing tooth or need to have a tooth pulled, our Costa Mesa dentists may be able to replace that tooth with a dental bridge. A dental bridge is used when a tooth can’t be salvaged with a filling or crown, but when there are still intact teeth that can be used to support your artificial replacement tooth. Much like it sounds, a bridge is a tooth that is firmly affixed between two stable teeth (as opposed to a dental implant, which is attached to your jaw with an artificial root). The bridging tooth is made to match your other teeth, giving you a complete row of teeth and confident smile.
To learn more about dental bridges and how they may be able to restore your smile, call us at 714.422.0596 or click here to schedule an appointment today.

headings.text: Our Cosmetic Dentists In Costa Mesa, CA, Can Improve Your Smile With Teeth Whitening

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

A bright set of teeth can give you a great reason to smile a little wider. If you wish your teeth were a bit whiter for an upcoming event such as a wedding, graduation, or job interview, or if you simply want to feel better about your smile, then schedule an appointment with one of our 5-star-rated cosmetic dentists in Costa Mesa, CA, for a professional teeth whitening treatment.

Teeth whitening with our Costa Mesa cosmetic dentists can help to remove years of stains caused by certain habits (like smoking) and the consumption of certain acidic foods and beverages (such as wine and chocolate). Unlike with over-the-counter teeth whitening kits, our dentists closely oversee your teeth whitening treatment to ensure that you won’t suffer any unpleasant or dangerous side effects, such as overly weakened tooth enamel or permanently sensitive teeth. In addition, our professional teeth whitening treatment can usually get your teeth even brighter than over-the-counter treatment – and in less time, too.

pages.title: Root Canal - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: Oral Surgeon In Costa Mesa, CA, For Root Canals

Unfortunately, the term “root canal” has a terrible reputation, but you can feel assured that your root canal at OC Healthy Smiles will be pain-free. A root canal is a procedure used to salvage a tooth by removing an infected and damaged root nerve. The surgery involves gently removing the nerve and pulp to prevent further damage to the surrounding teeth and gum. OC Healthy Smiles has a trained endodontist on staff who specializes in root canals, and if you have a busy weekday schedule, we can even arrange for your root canal to be performed on a Saturday.

To learn more about root canal procedures with our oral surgeon in Costa Mesa, CA, schedule an appointment with our endodontic specialist today. Call us at 714.422.0596 or click here to use our online appointment request form. We look forward to hearing from you!

pages.title: Cavities / Filling - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: OC Healthy Smiles sets the gold standard for patient experience

Sometimes cavities happen, even if you are careful to maintain good oral hygiene practices such as brushing, flossing, and visiting your family dentist for regular dental cleanings. When tooth decay sets in, our Orange County, CA, expert dentists use composite, mercury-free dental fillings to stop a cavity in its tracks.

If a cavity isn’t treated quickly and properly, it can lead to further tooth decay that can affect other teeth and the health of your gums as well. OC Healthy Smiles offers the latest in technological advancements that allow our doctors to safely and remove any tooth decay from a cavity and then fill the empty spot with a composite resin that will keep any food or bacteria from entering the tooth and causing more problems.

If you are concerned that you may have a cavity, or if you have not had a dental cleaning and exam in more than six months, then call our friendly team at 714.422.0596 or click here to schedule an appointment with one of our highly experienced Costa Mesa dentists. We can help you keep your healthy smile beaming for years to come!

pages.title: Dental Implants - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: Our Dentists In Costa Mesa, CA, Can Improve The Overall Look Of Your Teeth With The Most Effective Dental Implants

At OC Healthy Smiles, our mission is to provide the finest dental care, including quality dental implants in Costa Mesa, CA. If some or all of your teeth are no longer salvageable or are missing altogether, dental implants may be able to help you restore biting and chewing function, and in some cases they may even help to improve your speech. Dental implants are permanent artificial teeth that are custom created to match the shape and color of the rest of your teeth. This ensures that they are completely natural looking, and no one will ever suspect that you have artificial teeth unless you tell them yourself!

Dental implants are comfortable and feel much like your normal teeth do in your mouth. Dental implants are fixed in place with an artificial root or roots, which your gum tissue and jaw will accept like natural teeth. Unlike with a dental bridge, a dental implant doesn’t require the support of the surrounding teeth to stabilize it. At OC Healthy Smiles, we use only the safest, most effective dental implants available, and we use CT-scan-guided surgery to help ensure your ideal outcome. Because we use anesthesia, the implant procedure itself is, and any discomfort during the healing process can typically be managed with over-the-counter medication such as Ibuprofen (Advil).

If you are looking for a long-lasting solution for your missing teeth that acts, looks, and feels like your natural teeth, then contact us at OC Healthy Smiles to learn more. Call 714.422.0596 or click here to schedule an appointment today.

pages.title: Dental Sealants - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Costa Mesa Dentists For Dental Sealants

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Your molars (the teeth in the back of your mouth) have lots of ridges that are great for chewing. Unfortunately, these ridges can get worn down, and they can get deeper and narrower over time. When this happens, those grooves can become great hiding places for food particles and bacteria to hide. As a result, despite a thorough brushing and flossing regimen, you could end up with a cavity.

Happily, our OC Healthy Smiles dentists can provide you with a treatment called a dental sealant to help prevent food and bacteria from sneaking into those narrow spaces. Dental sealants are comprised of a resin that fills the worn spaces and creates a smoother surface for brushing away food and bacteria. By getting dental sealants placed on your molars, you can help to keep cavities and dental fillings at bay.

pages.title: Dental Crowns - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: Our Dentists In Costa Mesa, CA, Can Improve The Overall Look Of Your Teeth With The Most Effective Dental Crowns

Dental crowns help to strengthen and retain teeth that are suffering from tooth decay, extensive cavities, or extreme wear and tear from teeth grinding and other causes. This type of treatment is called a crown, because it sits atop your tooth in much the same way a crown would sit on a king’s head. A dental crown covers the top of your tooth, strengthens the integrity of the tooth’s overall structure, and provides a seal to keep any food or germs from getting into your tooth’s roots or gum tissue.

At OC Healthy Smiles, we specialize in non-metallic Lava Crowns, which are made of high-quality medical ceramics. Our non-metallic dental crowns are able to be matched to the color of your other teeth, and their strong ceramic materials ensures that your tooth will retain its full chewing and biting function for years to come.
Contact us at OC Healthy Smiles to learn more about how dental crowns can improve your smile. Call 714.422.0596 or click here to schedule an appointment today.

pages.title: Gum Treatment - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Fight Periodontal Disease With Our 5-star-rated Dentists For Painless Gum Therapy Treatment In Orange County, Ca
How Do I Avoid Periodontal Disease?
How Do I Know If I Have Periodontal Disease?

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Gum therapy, also sometimes called Scaling and Root Planing (SRP), treats the gum tissue around your teeth when they become inflamed or infected. Our dentists are highly experienced at providing painless gum therapy treatment in Orange County, CA, to patients who suffer from periodontal disease.

Periodontal disease, aka gum disease, is what we call an infection that affects the tissue surrounding your teeth. When periodontal disease isn’t treated promptly, it can lead to the loss of teeth and other problems with your jaw and facial appearance.

Usually, you can keep gum disease at bay by brushing at least twice a day, flossing once a day, and seeing one of our Orange County dentists twice a year for a dental cleaning and oral exam. If your gums are showing early signs of gum disease, we can give you advice and possibly treatment to help restore your gums to full health before your teeth are negatively affected.
By visiting our Orange County dentists for regular checkups twice a year, we can let you know if you have gum disease or are at risk of developing gum disease.
You should also call us for an immediate appointment if you notice any of the following signs of gum disease:

pages.title: Oral Cancer Screening - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Costa Mesa Dentists For Oral Cancer Screening

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Over 40,000 Americans are diagnosed with oral and throat cancers each year, and as with any type of cancer, the chance of recovery relies in part upon how early the cancer was first detected. As part of your general dental exam and dental cleaning, our Costa Mesa dentists perform a thorough screening for signs of oral cancer. When you visit our dental office, our dentist will perform a visual inspection of your mouth and a physical examination of your jaw and neck. Should any warning signs of cancer present themselves, we will then recommend that you promptly schedule an appointment with your primary physician. By helping to facilitate early cancer detection, our dentists and dental team at OC Healthy Smiles work toward more than just the health of your teeth and gums!

pages.title: Dentures - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Our Dentists In Costa Mesa, CA, Can Improve The Overall Look Of Your Teeth With Dentures

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

If you are missing too many teeth for a dental bridge but don’t feel confident about proceeding with dental implants, then dentures may be a good choice for you. Dentures are a set of removable artificial teeth that look and function naturally when you’re wearing them. If you’ve lost all of your natural teeth, whether from gum disease, tooth decay or injury, replacing missing teeth will benefit your appearance and your health. That’s because dentures make it easier to eat and speak better than you could without teeth – things that people often take for granted.

Our expert dentists in Costa Mesa, CA, and the OC Healthy Smiles team will take careful impressions to ensure that your new dentures will fit comfortable and securely. Make an appointment with us today, and let us give you another great reason to smile!

pages.title: Tooth Extraction - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Oral Surgeons In Costa Mesa, CA, For Wisdom Tooth Extraction

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Wisdom teeth are the third molars that begin to appear in late adulthood. When these teeth are in their proper place, all is well. For many people, however, wisdom teeth can become painful when they begin to erupt, because they tend to crowd other teeth, eventually leading to other problems.

At OC Healthy Smiles, we never want you or your child to be in pain. We often recommend oral surgery for wisdom tooth extraction – both to alleviate the discomfort of impacted wisdom teeth and to ensure that all the rest of the teeth come in straight. Regular monitoring of the wisdom teeth can keep you or your child from requiring further dental corrections later in life.

If you believe that you or your child needs wisdom tooth extraction, or if another dentist has recommend that you see a specialist about your wisdom teeth, then schedule an appointment with our expert oral surgeon in Costa Mesa, CA. Call us at 714.422.0596 or click here to schedule your appointment today!

pages.title: Fluoride Treatment - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Costa Mesa Dentists For Fluoride Treatment

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office

Fluoride is a mineral that can help to prevent cavities by repairing damaged tooth enamel when the damage is still in its very early, microscopic stages. The benefits of fluoride have been known for decades, which is why we typically brush our teeth with a fluoride-based toothpaste.

In-office fluoride treatment at our Costa Mesa dental office can also help you and your loved ones to protect the health and integrity of your teeth. The fluoride paste used at OC Healthy Smiles is a much higher concentration than what you will find in your toothpaste or mouth rinse, and our professional supervision ensures that this higher level of fluoride won’t cause any undue side effects. If you are at a higher risk of cavities due to genetics or any other reason, ask our dental team whether fluoride treatment would be a good option for you and your smile.

headings.text: Top Provider Of Invisalign In Orange County, CA

paragraphs: OUR 5-STAR-RATED COSTA MESA DENTAL OFFICE

The quality of your Invisalign treatment depends in part on the expertise of your dental provider. As the top Invisalign provider among Orange County dentists, OC Healthy Smiles provides you with expert care to ensure the best possible outcome for your ideal smile. At our Costa Mesa dental office, you can straighten your teeth for as little as $69 a month without your friends or family being any the wiser.

While you straighten your teeth with Invisalign, our Costa Mesa dental office can also shorten your overall treatment time thanks to the acceleration provided by Propel. Propel’s innovative devices and procedures allow your teeth to shift much more quickly than with Invisalign alone – in some cases, up to 50% faster!

To learn more about Invisalign and to find out if you are a candidate, schedule your appointment with one of our top-rated Orange County dentists at OC Healthy Smiles. paragraphs: We Offer Flexible Payment Options and Patient Financing

At OC Healthy Smiles, we never want you to put off necessary dental care for financial reasons. If you have dental insurance, we will work with your company to maximize your insurance benefits. If any balance remains, or if you don’t have dental insurance, we have paired up with CareCredit to offer our patients low-interest monthly payment options. We also offer our own in-house Smile Saver Dental Plan for patients who don’t have dental insurance. At OC Healthy Smiles, you don’t have to sacrifice expert dental care for affordable treatment!

paragraphs: Our 5-star-rated Costa Mesa Dental Office
5-Star Rated Orange County Dentists Serving Costa Mesa, Newport Beach, Newport Coast, Irvine, Fountain Valley, Corona del Mar, Huntington Beach, Tustin, Laguna Beach, Santa Ana, California (CA)

Sometimes our patients do a better job than we do at describing how much we care about providing top-quality dental care with a smile to everyone who walks through the doors of OC Healthy Smiles. See for yourself what others have been saying about our Orange County dentists!
5-Star Rated Dentist in Orange County, CA

The staff is extremely friendly and helpful here. This was my very first visit here and they welcomed me with a small gift for their first timers. They provide helpful advice. The actual facility itself is super clean, modern, and comfy.

Best dentist I’ve ever been to. Initially came in here for my wisdom tooth to be removed back in March where I felt no pain the process was as smooth as it could be. I just came back for my second treatment that I needed to get done today. Dr. Nguyen & Stephanie & their whole staff are always so nice and welcoming as soon as you walk in. Definitely recommend this place. I’ve never felt a pinch of pain since I’ve been here! Thank you for being so attentive.
Ps. Dr Nguyen checks up on you after your procedures which is so nice to have someone who cares not just about your money but making sure your experience is good.

I have been coming to this Orange County dental office for about a year and a half. The staff are very welcoming and friendly. For teeth cleanings they are very gentle on your teeth and gums. The office is nice and modern. They always offer you water and goodie bag at the end. Would recommend. Best Orange County dentist I’ve ever had

Absolute best Orange County dentist I’ve ever been to and I’ve been to many! They give great service, remember you by name and make you feel at ease. If you are looking for a place, come here you won’t regret it.

pages.title: Emergency Appointments - OC Healthy Smiles

headings.text: Emergency Appointments With Our Orange County Dentists

paragraphs: OUR 5-STAR-RATED COSTA MESA DENTAL OFFICE

Dental pain can quickly ruin your day or even your week when it strikes. Our Orange County dentists at OC Healthy Smiles believe that when you’re in pain, you should be able to get relief as quickly as possible. This is why we reserve time in our schedule for short-notice emergency appointments – and we make sure these appointments are long enough to actually treat your pain, not simply evaluate it! If you have a cavity, we will take care of the decay and get it filled right away. If your resolution needs additional work, we will take care of as much as we can the same day to bring you relief, and then we’ll take impressions to prepare any necessary customized solutions, which will provide you with a permanent solution in your follow-up appointment. Ultimately, our goal at your dental appointment is to find the problem, relieve the pain, and prepare for a solution.

We offer white fillings as they just look more natural than silver fillings. How much is teeth whitening? We have two options. For in office we use GLO whitening which is $475 and the session is 90 minutes. The take home trays are $135 and they come with 2 whitening gel tubes; you apply for 5-14 days for 30 minutes a day. What is the estimate for veneers? Veneers start at $2050. I’m pregnant and want to get my teeth cleaned before my baby is here. Do you do teeth cleanings without x rays? Yes.To protect our patients and their unborn child, a medical release from your OBGYN is required for pregnant women before any dental visits. What type of implants do you use? Our implants are customized to your unique situation and are made out of biocompatible titanium. Do we do oral surgery? paragraphs: Educational Videos By Your Oc Healthy Smiles Dental Team

At OC Healthy Smiles, our mission is to provide the finest affordable dental care in Orange County, CA. For more information on teeth whitening, cavities dental implants, or oral hygiene, watch our videos below.
Did you know that crooked teeth are bad for your health? Learn more from OC Healthy Smiles hygienist, Alexis!
We know you hear about it at every visit, but how important is flossing? Your OC Healthy Smiles hygienist, Tanya, breaks down if flossing is important and how to floss properly.

When you get your teeth professionally whitened, you want it to last as long as possible. Your OC Healthy Smiles hygienist, Tanya, put together a list of 3 ways to help your smile brilliant and white longer.
Bad breath can hinder your confidence or relationships, not to mention it could mean you have a dental issue. Use these 4 tips to get rid of your bad breath and on to a path of better oral health.

Have you ever noticed “pink in the sink” while brushing? If you have and just shrugged it off, your OC Healthy Smiles hygienist, Tanya, is here to explain why finding even just a little blood in the sink while brushing is something to take seriously.

pages.title: Invisalign Questionnaire - OC Healthy Smiles
paragraphs: INVISALIGN PRE-CONSULT QUESTIONNAIRE
Our dentists have over 15 years of experience with Invisalign and have been awarded with the title of Platinum Invisalign Providers.

Let’s get to know your smile! We will be customizing your Invisalign consult based on YOUR concerns. Please complete this short questionnaire prior to your appointment. See below for more information on Invisalign.
Price of Invisalign-

pages.title: Cancellation Policy - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: CANCELLATION & NO-SHOW POLICY To help us provide the best care to all our patients, we ask that you make every effort to keep your reserved appointment time. We do not double-book, so your time is set aside just for you. Appointment Confirmations: You’ll receive a reminder via email, text, or phone call one week
paragraphs: To help us provide the best care to all our patients, we ask that you make every effort to keep your reserved appointment time. We do not double-book, so your time is set aside just for you.

You’ll receive a reminder via email, text, or phone call one week prior to your visit. Please confirm your appointment by responding through your preferred method. If we don’t hear from you at least 24 business hours in advance, your appointment may be released to accommodate other patients awaiting care.
You may cancel or reschedule your appointment up to 24 hours in advance without a fee. By providing your credit card information, you agree to the following fees if canceling late or failing to show:
If you are more than 10 minutes late, we may need to reschedule your appointment to avoid delays for other patients. A $75 broken appointment fee may apply unless the delay is due to an emergency.

You can cancel your appointment by text, email, or by calling us at (714) 545-0484. While we’d much rather see you, we appreciate your understanding and cooperation in helping us maintain smooth scheduling.

headings.text: Are You Ruining Your Health With Crooked Teeth?
5 Solutions to Worn and Aging Teeth

paragraphs: Posted December 17, 2019 in Uncategorized

Contest Rules: Eligibility: All eligibility requirements associated with the Official Rules of OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway applies. All Entrants must be (18) years or older and legal residents of the United States and be able to travel to OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA to receive the $10K Smile Giveaway. Overview Patients […]
Posted June 17, 2019 in Uncategorized

Crooked teeth impact more aspects of your life than you realize and can deteriorate your overall health. Here are a few common side effects of having misaligned teeth. Bad Breath Crooked teeth make it difficult to have proper oral hygiene because hard to reach crevices harbor food particles and bacteria. The left behind food particles […]
Posted November 28, 2018 in Uncategorized

If your smile is more dingy yellow than pearly white or you have noticed that your teeth are shifting, don’t worry. You don’t have to grin and bear the bad news. Although discolored teeth, receding gums, and chips and gaps are signs of aging teeth, there are many things you can do to restore your […]
Posted October 31, 2018 in Uncategorized

Bad breath, also known as halitosis, affects 50 percent of adults at some point in their lives. It can cause embarrassment and anxiety which is why so many companies have created minty products to temporarily mask bad breath. If you are experiencing symptoms, here are 4 tips to get rid of bad breath. Brush and […]
Posted July 25, 2018 in Uncategorized

The American Dental Association (ADA) found that 22% of patients don’t go to the dentist out of fear. That is more than 1 in 5 people! At OC healthy Smiles, we are committed to our patient’s needs and are thoughtful of those who may have dental fears. We offer a number of relaxation options to […]

OUR ADDRESS AND PHONE NUMBER ARE BELOW: OC HEALTHY SMILES 1202 Bristol St #140, Costa Mesa, CA 92626 Phone: 714.422.0596 Email: info@ochealthysmiles.net This privacy policy discloses the privacy practices for (www.ochealthysmiles.net). This privacy policy applies solely to information collected by this web site. It will notify you of the following: 1. What personally identifiable information is collected from you through the web site, how it is used and with whom it may be shared. 2. What choices are available to you regarding the use of your data. 3. The security procedures in place to protect the misuse of your information. 4. How you can correct any inaccuracies in the information. Our website uses cookies. By using our website and agreeing to this policy, you consent to our use of cookies in accordance with the terms of this policy. Information Collection, Use, and Sharing We are the sole owners of the information collected on this site. We may collect, store and use the following kinds of personal information: 1. information about your computer and about your visits to and use of this website (including your IP address, geographical location, browser type and version, operating system, referral source, length of visit, page views, website navigation); 2. information relating to any transactions carried out between you and us on or in relation to this website, including information relating to any purchases you make of our goods or services; 3. information that you provide to us for the purpose of registering with us; 4. information that you provide to us for the purpose of subscribing to our website services, email notifications and/or newsletters; and 5. any other information that you choose to send to us; Before you disclose to us the personal information of another person, you must obtain that person’s consent to both the disclosure and the processing of that personal information in accordance with the terms of this privacy policy. We will use your information to respond to you, regarding the reason you contacted us. We may share your information with another third party outside of our organization, if necessary to fulfill your request (e.g. to ship an order) or to facilitate our organization in managing our website. Unless you ask us not to, we may contact you via email in the future to tell you about specials, or new products or services. Your Access to and Control Over Information You may opt out of any future contacts from us at any time. You can do the following at any time by contacting us via the email address or phone number given on our website: 1. See what data we have about you, if any. 2. Change/correct any data we have about you. 3. Have us delete any data we have about you. 4. Express any concern you have about our use of your data. Security We will take reasonable technical and organizational precautions to prevent the loss, misuse or alteration of your personal information. We will store all the personal information you provide on our secure (password and firewall-protected) servers. Only employees who need the information to perform a specific job (for example, billing or customer service) are granted access to personally identifiable information. The computers/servers in which we store personally identifiable information are kept in a secure environment. Orders We may accept orders and if we do we request information from you on our order form. To buy from us, you must provide contact information (like name and shipping address) and financial information (like credit card number, expiration date). This information is used for billing purposes and to fill your orders. If we have trouble processing an order, we’ll use this information to contact you. Cookies We use “cookies” on this site. A cookie is a piece of data stored on a site visitor’s hard drive to help us improve your access to our site and identify repeat visitors to our site. For instance, when we use a cookie to identify you, you would not have to log in a password more than once, thereby saving time while on our site. Cookies can also enable us to track and target the interests of our users to enhance the experience on our site. Some of our business partners may use cookies on our site (for example, advertisers). However, we have no access to or control over these cookies. We do not honor the Do Not Track (DNT) web browser privacy preference at this time. Sharing We share aggregated demographic information with our partners and advertisers. And: We may use an outside shipping company to ship orders, and a credit card processing company to bill users for goods and services. These companies do not retain, share, store or use personally identifiable information for any secondary purposes beyond filling your order. And: We may partner with another party to provide specific services. We will share names, or other contact information that is necessary for the third party to provide these services. These parties are not allowed to use personally identifiable information except for the purpose of providing these services. Disclosures We may disclose your personal information to any of our employees, officers, agents, suppliers or subcontractors insofar as reasonably necessary for the purposes set out in this privacy policy. We may disclose your personal information to any member of our group of companies (this means our subsidiaries, our ultimate holding company and all its subsidiaries) insofar as reasonably necessary for the purposes set out in this privacy policy. In addition, we may disclose your personal information: 1. to the extent that we are required to do so by law; 2. in connection with any ongoing or prospective legal proceedings; 3. in order to establish, exercise or defend our legal rights (including providing information to others for the purposes of fraud prevention and reducing credit risk); 4. to the purchaser (or prospective purchaser) of any business or asset that we are (or are contemplating) selling; and 5. to any person who we reasonably believe may apply to a court or other competent authority for disclosure of that personal information where, in our reasonable opinion, such court or authority would be reasonably likely to order disclosure of that personal information. Links This web site contains links to other sites. Please be aware that we are not responsible for the content or privacy practices of such other sites. We encourage our users to be aware when they leave our site and to read the privacy statements of any other site that collects personally identifiable information. Surveys & Contests From time-to-time our site requests information via surveys or contests. Participation in these surveys or contests is

pages.title: 5 Solutions to Worn and Aging Teeth - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: If your smile is more dingy yellow than pearly white or you have noticed that your teeth are shifting, don’t worry. You don’t have to grin and bear the bad news. Although discolored teeth, receding gums, and chips and gaps are signs of aging teeth, there are many things you can do to restore your

headings.text: 5 Solutions to Worn and Aging Teeth
SOLUTION #2: NIGHT GUARDS AND CLEAR ALIGNERS

paragraphs: Posted November 28, 2018 in Uncategorized

If your smile is more dingy yellow than pearly white or you have noticed that your teeth are shifting, don’t worry. You don’t have to grin and bear the bad news. Although discolored teeth, receding gums, and chips and gaps are signs of aging teeth, there are many things you can do to restore your sparkling smile.

Teeth have a tough job, and not everyone likes the look of the effects of decades of wear and tear. According to dentists, the top aging teeth issues include discoloration, crowding or gaps, and loss of enamel. Dr. Mark Nguyen of OC Healthy Smiles says that loss of enamel, the outermost and protective layer of your teeth, results in both darker and shorter teeth, and shorter teeth can move. That leads to a whole host of other issues, he says, including crowding, overlapping, and gaps.

Gum recession is also common among older adults. “When your gums recede, the roots of the teeth can be exposed,” says Dr. Nguyen. Not only does it look unappealing, but it can lead to tooth damage or tooth loss if left untreated. Tackling these age-related issues usually requires a multipronged approach that takes into account aesthetic, functional, and health concerns.

“If your teeth are still in good shape, think prevention,” says Dr. Mark Nguyen, who, for two consecutive years, has been voted by board-certified California dentists as Top Cosmetic Dentist in Orange Coast Magazine. His recommendations — in addition to good daily oral hygiene and professional teeth cleaning every three to six months — is to avoid acidic foods like coffee, tea, sparkling water, soda, and red wine. It’s also a good idea to brush well after eating foods such as blueberries and beets, which can stain teeth. And curb bad habits like biting fingernails and chewing ice, Dr. Nguyen says.

It may not be the prettiest thing to wear to bed, but dentists often recommend a night guard to protect teeth from grinding while you sleep. “Grinding and clenching of teeth is a huge concern with aging teeth,” says Dr. Nguyen. “When you grind and clench you create a situation where the enamel wears away. It’s similar to rubbing your teeth with a very fine sandpaper over the years. You thin out the edges and teeth chip or wear away.” Grinding and clenching can also move teeth, leading to spacing or crowding.

For teeth that have already shifted, Invisalign clear aligners can give you a brighter, straighter smile. This clear aligner systems can work faster than traditional braces if coupled with an experienced dentist, like those at OC Healthy Smiles, and propel technology. The propel technology can safely correct your smile up to 50% faster, so if timing is a concern, be sure to ask about this treatment option. Though that might sound like a great idea, every case is different. Do your homework before you make a commitment. Most dentists offer free Invisalign consultations, so be sure to schedule one to get all your questions answered.

One of the more common ways to remedy darkening or yellowing teeth is whitening. For external stains, Dr. Nguyen recommends over-the-counter teeth-whitening products include strips, gels, and rinses. They can take anywhere from a few hours to a few weeks to see results. The cost range is usually between $15 to $50.

For internal tooth stains, however, you may want to schedule a professional whitening at your dentist’s office or have a dentist make custom whitening trays that you can wear at night. The in-office process is more dramatic and much faster than over the counter methods because it involves a stronger bleaching agent and laser or heat light to boost results. The good news is that it takes only 90 minutes. The enamel-safe whitening agent used by dentists will brighten your natural teeth, but tooth-colored fillings, crowns, veneers, bonding, and bridges don’t whiten, so be sure to discuss previous dental work with your practitioner if you go this route.

To fix the shape of your teeth or to repair broken, uneven teeth, you have essentially two options: bonding and veneers. Bonding is typically used to repair small cracks and chips in teeth. It’s fairly affordable, but it can easily darken and chip. Veneers are the gold standard for tooth repair. “Veneers can be used to do more significant cosmetic enhancements and still look very natural,” says Dr. Nguyen

For gums that have receded, dentists and oral surgeons usually recommend gum grafting to cover the area of the tooth that is exposed. In the past, this was a pretty painful procedure, because tissue had to be harvested from the roof of the patient’s mouth. But there’s a new technique that Dr. Nguyen calls a game changer. The results are fantastic and the patient won’t have to worry about discomfort due to advancements in anesthetics and oral surgery.
For more information or to schedule now, call your dentist at OC Healthy Smiles (714) 545-0484

pages.title: $10,000 Smile Giveaway Rules - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: Contest Rules: Eligibility: All eligibility requirements associated with the Official Rules of OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway applies. All Entrants must be (18) years or older and legal residents of the United States and be able to travel to OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA to receive the $10K Smile Giveaway. Overview Patients
paragraphs: Posted December 17, 2019 in Uncategorized

Contest Rules: Eligibility: All eligibility requirements associated with the Official Rules of OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway applies. All Entrants must be (18) years or older and legal residents of the United States and be able to travel to OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA to receive the $10K Smile Giveaway. Overview Patients will have the opportunity to submit a video entry anytime between December 26, 2019, and January 15, 2020. The winner’s selection will be as follows: OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway contestants will be selected from eligible and approved entries (see Entry Requirements) by an independent panel of judges. These entries will be collected until January 15, 2020. The staff of OC Healthy Smiles will narrow the submissions down to (3) finalists. Each of the (3) finalists will receive a free exam from Dr. Mark k. Nguyen DDS at OC Healthy Smiles. After all (3) finalists have been examined, the OC Healthy Smiles team will pick the winner, who will receive up to $10,000 worth of dental work from OC Healthy Smiles. The winner will be announced on January 24, 2020. To Enter: Record a video and post it on YouTube titled: “OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway 2020” In the video, tell us why you deserve the $10K Smile or have someone do it for you The video should not exceed 3 minutes OR take a photograph of your smile or the smile of the person you are nominating and explain in 300 words or less why you/they are a good candidate for the giveaway. Email the link to the video or photo to sch@ochealthysmiles.net with the following information: Your name, Phone Number, City, State, Email address, Age, and names of any additional people that appear in the video.

All entries must adhere to the ENTRY REQUIREMENTS specified below. You may not submit more than one entry. No correspondence about entries will be entered into, nor will videos be acknowledged or returned. If required information is not included, Entry will be disqualified. 2 Entries that meet the Entry Requirements, Contest Requirements, and Conditions of Entry, specified below, may be posted on the social media site and used in advertising promotions. If you think that any Entry infringes on your intellectual property rights email sch@ochealthysmiles.net.
Entry Requirements:

Send an email to sch@ochealthysmiles.net that includes: Your name, Phone Number, City, State, Email address, Age, and names of any additional people that appear in the video. A title for your Entry AND A video submission- no more than 3 minutes long. A small 2-3 sentence introduction for your video submission. Include a link to your YouTube submission, which should be titled, “OC Healthy Smiles $10K Smile Giveaway 2020” OR A photograph of your smile and a short story (300 words or less) explaining why you are a good candidate for the giveaway. Tell us why you deserve the $10K Smile. Feel free to get creative. Pull heartstrings, make us laugh, tell us a story, have someone tell it for you…or simply upload the video from your cell phone. Ideas for your Story: Tell a personal story of your current smile, what you don’t like about it, how your current smile has affected you. Explain how a new smile would change your life. Show your current smile on the video (or photograph). Explain why you haven’t fixed your smile in the past. If you are nominating someone else in your video, you can show a picture that demonstrates their smile in your video. 3 Must not infringe on any 3rd party rights. Any people other than yourself must be identified by name and age. Once the video has been uploaded and the entry email has been received, the entry is final and cannot be modified, supplemented or edited. Entries that fail to adhere to any of the requirements set forth in these Official Terms and Conditions, including the requirements set forth in this section, as well as the requirements set forth in the official terms and conditions as determined by the Sponsors, and/or Contest Administrator, in their sole discretion, will be disqualified. Sponsors are not responsible for the late or incomplete entries. If a problem occurs during the online submission process or in uploading a video, the Entrant should receive an “error” Message. However, the responsibility to electronically complete the Entry process and upload a video is the Entrants and neither Sponsor nor Contest Administrator shall be responsible for any video not actually received due to incomplete or improper submission. Similarly, neither sponsor nor Contest Administrator has any obligation to advise the Entrant of an incomplete entry or video submission.
Content Requirements

If the video was taken professionally, the Entrant must have permission from the person/business that required said video. pages.metaDescription: Crooked teeth impact more aspects of your life than you realize and can deteriorate your overall health. Here are a few common side effects of having misaligned teeth. Bad Breath Crooked teeth make it difficult to have proper oral hygiene because hard to reach crevices harbor food particles and bacteria. The left behind food particles

headings.text: Are You Ruining Your Health With Crooked Teeth?

paragraphs: Posted June 17, 2019 in Uncategorized
Crooked teeth impact more aspects of your life than you realize and can deteriorate your overall health. Here are a few common side effects of having misaligned teeth.

Crooked teeth make it difficult to have proper oral hygiene because hard to reach crevices harbor food particles and bacteria. The left behind food particles causes more bacteria to breed and lessens your gum and tooth health. In severe cases, this can lead to periodontal and gum disease.
The ADA has conducted studies that show that build-up of bacteria from poor oral hygiene caused by misaligned teeth can lead to other systemic health issues.

Teeth that do not fit together properly tend to rub against each other causing the outer layer of the tooth to wear down. This increases your chances of cavities and other dental problems. Serious tooth wear leads to nerve exposure and becomes extremely painful.
Misaligned teeth may cause stress on your jaw joint. Stress on your jaw can cause inflammation and soreness to ensue. Furthermore, this can cause headaches or migraines if left untreated.

There’s also an emotional or psychological effect of having a crooked smile that shouldn’t be overlooked. After all, people who are more confident about their smile are less likely to be depressed which has major implications on your overall health as well.

Luckily, all of these issues can be lessened or prevented by realigning your teeth with Invisalign clear braces. As a VIP Platinium Provider of Invisalign, your OC Healthy Smiles team has had years of training to help you achieve your healthy and straight smile. View what your smile will look like using our Smile Simulator just click HERE or call (714) 545-0484 to schedule your FREE Invisalign consultation today!

pages.title: 4 Solutions to Bad Breath - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, affects 50 percent of adults at some point in their lives. paragraphs: Posted October 31, 2018 in Uncategorized

Bad breath, also known as halitosis, affects 50 percent of adults at some point in their lives. It can cause embarrassment and anxiety which is why so many companies have created minty products to temporarily mask bad breath. If you are experiencing symptoms, here are 4 tips to get rid of bad breath.
It is best to start off by thoroughly brushing your teeth and flossing. Flossing will help remove remaining bacteria and food particles out from between your teeth that could cause bad breath.
Next, clean your tongue with a tongue scraper. Your tongue harbors bacteria which, if not properly cared for, can cause bad breath as well.
For a quick fix, maybe after a garlic filled dinner, chew sugar-free gum. The sugar-free gum will freshen your breath and help remove bacteria from your mouth.

Be sure to drink plenty of water to prevent dry mouth. When you don’t produce enough saliva, it is harder to wash away bacteria and food particles that are stuck between your teeth. Staying hydrated and brushing regularly will help wash out the bacteria and food particles that linger.

Experiencing bad breath for a long period of time means that you could be encountering a more serious issue such as a sinus condition, gastric reflux, diabetes, liver or kidney disease. Call your OC Healthy Smiles dentist at (714) 545-0484 to have your dentist properly examine and treat your halitosis.

pages.title: Anxiety-Free Dental Sedation Options - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: The American Dental Association (ADA) found that 22% of patients don't go to the dentist out of fear. That is more than 1 in 5 people! At OC healthy Smiles, we are committed to our patient's needs and are thoughtful of those who may have dental fears. We offer a number of relaxation options to

headings.text: What Types of Dental Sedation Do You Offer?
How Safe Is Dental IV Sedation?

paragraphs: Posted July 25, 2018 in Uncategorized

The American Dental Association (ADA) found that 22% of patients don’t go to the dentist out of fear. That is more than 1 in 5 people! At OC healthy Smiles, we are committed to our patient’s needs and are thoughtful of those who may have dental fears.

We offer a number of relaxation options to calm your nerves during dental visits. Depending on your needs, you can request one of the many types of relaxation and dental sedation options that OC Healthy Smiles has to offer. To better understand the difference between each and to know which is best for your situation, make sure to schedule a consultation with us.

You can drive to and from your appointments safely with no major side effects with nitrous oxide or more commonly known as laughing gas. Breathing in this colorless and odorless gas will quickly relax you and when the gas is shut off, the effects will wear off in about 5 minutes. This is one of the most popular sedation methods used because it works so quickly and has little to no side effects. It is best to eat a light meal prior to using nitrous to avoid minor side effects such as nausea or headaches.

Most commonly used when seeing your oral surgeon, partial sedation will relax you while also being a pain blocker. With this method, you will be required to have a driver for after your appointment and it is suggested that you relax after sedation. Depending on your treatment, your doctor will go over more instructions for this method of sedation.

No matter how big or small the treatment, our in-office dental anesthesiologist is trained to safely administer IV sedation. With this method, you will be asleep for the entirety of the treatment. Dentists usually offer this method for patients with extreme dental fear and longer treatments. To learn more about IV sedation and how to schedule your appointment call OC Healthy Smiles at (714) 545-0484.

When you have an IV sedation at OC Healthy Smiles, you will be under the care of a dental anesthesiologist who will monitor your vitals through the entire procedure. Our dental anesthesiologist specializes in caring for patients with dental fears and will walk you through care instructions. Our specialist has gone through additional training and certifications to provide our patients with the best and safest care while they are sedated. The anesthesiologist tailors the amount of sedation to your specific needs and treatment, making the process extremely safe.

Your dentist can suggest one of the options for you based upon what type of treatment you will need. At all of your visits, we encourage you to take advantage of our comfort menu that is designed to keep you relaxed with offerings of items like calming lavender scented towels, headphones, or even Dish television to ease your mind.
Call (714) 545-0484 to schedule your visit to find out which option is best for you!
