pages.title: How To Care For Your Smile While Sick - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: Here are a few tips on how to keep up your oral health while you are sick. Brush Your Teeth When feeling under the weather, it is important to take care of your body including your mouth. It is essential that you continue to brush your teeth even when you are not feeling your best.

headings.text: How To Care For Your Smile While Sick

paragraphs: Posted January 15, 2018 in Uncategorized
Here are a few tips on how to keep up your oral health while you are sick.

When feeling under the weather, it is important to take care of your body including your mouth. It is essential that you continue to brush your teeth even when you are not feeling your best. Brushing will prevent bacteria from staying in your mouth and decrease your risk for future dental issues. Remember to replace your toothbrush every 3-4 months and to let your brush air dry. There is no need to replace a new toothbrush after sickness since your chances of getting re-infected is very low.

Cough drops are the go-to over the counter treatment when fighting a sore throat. Sugar-free cough drops have little to no side effects and contain vitamins and/or herbs that can additionally aid a cold. Sucking on these will increase saliva flow in your mouth which is helpful in flushing out bacteria. Some cough-drops taste just like candy. That is because these options contain sugar as their main ingredient. Sugar-filled cough drops are just as bad as sucking on candy. Cavity-causing bacteria thrive under these conditions. When choosing cough drops, opt for a sugar-free option to best protect your smile from cavities.

Staying hydrated while you are sick is not only good for your body, it is also helpful for your smile. Some of the medicine that you are taking while sick may dry your mouth out. Drinking water rather than other sugar drink options is healthier for your whole body especially your teeth. Water flushes out food particles and quenches a dry mouth. Dry mouth can increase your risk of cavities. It is imperative that you continue to drink water.

Unfortunately, with the flu and other sicknesses, you may find yourself vomiting. Always rinse your mouth and spit out any remaining residue. You should brush your teeth immediately after vomiting. The stomach acid that comes up with vomit coats your teeth and it could be harmful to your enamel if you don’t brush it off immediately. You can neutralize the acid by swishing and rinsing with a mixture of baking soda and water.

Whether you are sick now, or you are just preparing for the future, it is always important to keep up your oral hygiene. Your mouth is the gateway to your body and everything you put in your mouth affects your body. Call OC Healthy Smiles today at (714) 545-0484 to schedule your next exam and cleaning!

pages.metaDescription: Your mouth is the gateway to your body. As your dentist, it is our job to keep that gateway clean to ensure that you not only have a healthy smile, but also a healthy body. We have compiled a list of foods and beverages that you may consume daily that can help or hurt your

headings.text: What Are The Best And Worst Foods For Your Smile?

paragraphs: Posted November 29, 2017 in Uncategorized

Your mouth is the gateway to your body. As your dentist, it is our job to keep that gateway clean to ensure that you not only have a healthy smile, but also a healthy body. We have compiled a list of foods and beverages that you may consume daily that can help or hurt your smile.

Some of the usual culprits that affect the look and health of your smile include coffee, tea, soda, and red wine. According to the American Dental Association (ADA), coffees and teas can dry out your mouth and stain your teeth if consumed frequently. Soda and most carbonated drinks are very acidic, making them bad for your teeth since the acidity attacks and breaks down the enamel on your teeth. Red wine and alcohol reduce the saliva flow overtime which can lead to a multitude of dental problems. The most common problem from reduced saliva in the mouth is oral infections and gum disease. Red wine is known to stain your teeth if frequently consumed.

Here are some daily tips on lessening damage to your teeth. Use a straw to decrease the exposure of your teeth to any of these beverages. If you do drink coffee, tea or soda, drink plenty of water to help your mouth re-hydrate. Keep your coffee additives such as sugar and cream to a minimum.

Some foods are good for our body, but not for our teeth. If you know that a certain food or drink will stain your clothing, then it is most likely that it will also stain your teeth. Blueberries, pomegranates, and blackberries are full of healthy antioxidants but are a major contributor to staining your smile. White wine may not stain your teeth like red wine, but it does have properties to make the current stains on your teeth harder to get out.

Just because these foods stain your smile does not mean that you have to completely cut them out of your diet. When you do consume any of these listed items, it is important to brush and floss to keep any residue from sticking around your mouth longer than necessary. If you are suffering from the effects of these consumables, then whitening or other treatment may be necessary. Call (714) 422-0596 for an exam with one of our of dentists to see the plan that is best for you.

Cheese, yogurt, and almonds are full of calcium and protein, making them all beneficial for the health and strength the enamel of your teeth. When you consume cheese, your mouth increases saliva production and raises your oral pH level. Both of these factors contribute to a lower risk of tooth decay. The natural bacteria found in plain yogurt is great for your gums since it will replace the cavity causing bad bacteria that currently inhabit your gums.

An apple a day just might keep your dentist at bay since this sweet treat is great at keeping your pearly whites clean. The apple texture stimulates your gums and prompts your mouth to produce more saliva. The extra saliva helps rinse away trapped food particles and bacteria from your smile. Apples are nature’s toothbrush, but do not replace brushing your teeth completely.

Make your mouth and body happy by having good oral hygiene. It is important to see your dentist every 6 months and to regularly brush and floss your teeth. Get your oral health back on track by calling Costa Mesa’s best dentists at OC Healthy Smiles at (714) 422-0596.

We are your all-in-one dental office in Costa Mesa, CA!

pages.title: How Do I Check My Dental Insurance Benefits? - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: One of the great things we do for our patients is provide a complimentary benefits check. While you are able to call your insurance company to obtain your dental benefit coverage, sometimes patients feel confused by the insurance company jargon. We are here to help and educate each and every one of our patients. One

headings.text: How Do I Check My Dental Insurance Benefits?
Finding Your Benefits Card And Plan Information
What Is The Difference Between PPO And HMO?
Will My Dental Exam And Cleaning Be Payed For?
Still Have Questions About Your Benefits?
What Will I Need For A Benefits Check?

paragraphs: Posted November 20, 2017 in Uncategorized

One of the great things we do for our patients is provide a complimentary benefits check. While you are able to call your insurance company to obtain your dental benefit coverage, sometimes patients feel confused by the insurance company jargon. We are here to help and educate each and every one of our patients. One of our goals at OC Healthy Smiles is to improve our patients’ lives through education, because in the end, it will help to improve your overall health.

First, find your dental insurance card for your plan. Make sure you are looking at your card for your dental insurance. Medical insurance cards usually don’t usually include dental benefits. Identify your insurance provider with your insurance card and visit your provider’s website. If the provider website allows, sign in to your account and view your current plan benefits. The provider site should have a variety of services to help you learn more about what they offer. If you don’t get enough information from the website, call your insurance and ask to speak to a representative. The representative should be able to let you know if your insurance is PPO, HMO, or a hybrid plan and what your insurance covers.

HMO plans require that you use a doctor who is contracted with the insurance company. PPOs typically allow you to use any doctor or provider. If an HMO patient chooses to go to a doctor that is out of network, it is likely that the insurance will not pay for this visit. OC Healthy Smiles does not want to compromise the quality of care of patients and for that reason, are considered out of network with HMO insurances. PPO plans allow you more flexibility when choosing a provider and specialists.

General exam and cleanings are considered “preventative” care by insurance companies. You will need to check the percentage that the insurance company will pay. If you see that your insurance will cover part of the fees, you would be responsible for the remaining percentage. For example, if it says “80% of preventative” is covered, then you would be responsible for 20% of the exam and cleaning fees.
If you have further questions about your benefits and what your insurance covers, we strongly advise you to give your insurance company a call so they can explain your benefits for you.

We are considered a Premiere Provider with Delta Dental and in-network with Aetna PPO and Guardian PPO. We work with most PPO dental insurances. We don’t accept HMO plans at this time. By not altering our fees based on insurance coverage, we are able to use the best quality labs and dental materials to produce the finest solutions for your dental needs. Our goal is to improve our patients’ lives through quality care, compassion, and education. If you still have questions about your insurance and if our office accepts your plan, then please give our office a call at (714) 422-0596 and we will perform a complimentary benefits check for you.

We will need you to provide us with: the Policy Holder’s Name, Date of Birth, Member ID/Social Security Number, Insurance Company Name/ Insurance Company Phone Number, Employer Name (if the employer supplies insurance).
*Please allow 2 business days for an accurate benefits check.
2900 Bristol st. A205 Costa Mesa, CA 92626
Monday – Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM Friday – Sunday: Closed

Dr. Nguyen and his award-winning Costa Mesa, CA dentists, Costa Mesa, CA cosmetic dentists and Costa Mesa, CA dentists offer 5-star rated and comfortable dental care for children and adults, including teeth cleanings, dental implants, veneers, teeth whitening, Invisalign and more. We are your all-in-one dental office in Costa Mesa, CA!

Call 714.422.0596 today for your OC Healthy Smiles dental checkup at our conveniently located office in Costa Mesa, CA. pages.metaDescription: A message from Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS: "At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer a technique called CT scan guided implant surgery. We are one one of the few offices in Orange County that has invested in this technology to safely and effectively to do implant surgery. Many patients are more satisfied with the results of

headings.text: What Is The Difference Between A Dental Implant Or A Bridge?

paragraphs: Posted November 22, 2017 in Uncategorized
A message from Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS:

“At OC Healthy Smiles, we offer a technique called CT scan guided implant surgery. We are one one of the few offices in Orange County that has invested in this technology to safely and effectively to do implant surgery. Many patients are more satisfied with the results of an implant rather than a bridge. Reasons to consider a dental implant over a bridge; a bridge requires a dentist to carve down some of the tooth structure on the two healthy teeth next to the missing tooth. An implant how ever, only requires work in the space where the missing tooth was. Another reason patient prefer implants is that a dental implant is much easier to keep clean than a bridge. And lastly, patients prefer implants because a dental implant is much more natural looking than a bridge.”

When replacing a missing tooth, it is likely that you will have to make the choice between a dental implant or a bridge. When faced with these options, it is always beneficial to be informed on some key facts about bridges and implants. The skilled dentists at OC Healthy Smiles, have been practicing for many years and use the most advanced technology for all of our patients. To make the process easier, we have put together some key information on both options so you can make an informed decision.

Bridges have been around for hundreds of years and have advanced, especially in the past 30 years. To replace the missing tooth, a bridge requires a dentist to carve down some of the tooth structure on the two healthy teeth next to the missing tooth. The teeth are then replaced with three or more connected crowns. The design of the crown effectively bridges the gap between teeth, but also makes brushing slightly more complicated. For good oral health, patients with bridges must use special brushing and flossing techniques to keep food particles and bacteria from being trapped. Depending on how well you take care of your bridge, they can last anywhere from 8 years to a lifetime.

It has been found that many patients are more satisfied with the results of a dental implant rather than a bridge. An implant only requires the dentist to work with the space where the missing tooth was. The implant is more natural looking than a bridge and can be color matched to your enamel. OC Healthy Smiles offers a technique called CT Scan Guided Implant surgery and is one of the few offices in Orange County that has invested in this technology to safely and effectively perform implant surgery. The implant looks, feels, and appears like a natural tooth, so your daily brushing and flossing routine does not need to be changed to accommodate the implant. Implants are easier to keep clean and are less likely to need to be replaced because of this.

Every patient has a unique smile, so make sure to schedule an appointment with us to find out if a bridge or implant is the right option for your missing tooth. Feel free to call OC Healthy Smiles to schedule your exam with one of our expert dentists at (714) 422-0596 or to get more information on dental implants and bridges.

pages.title: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays? - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays? Dental x-rays allow your doctor to identify decay in areas that may not be visible in an oral exam. These x-rays are key in maintaining proper oral health and can help your dentist identify dental problems in early stages that the naked

headings.text: Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays?
Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays?
I Do Not Want My Loved Ones To Be Exposed To Too Much Radiation. How Do I Lower The Radiation Effect, And Maintain Good Oral Health?
How Often Should I Have an X-Ray of My Teeth?

paragraphs: Posted November 13, 2017 in Uncategorized

Dental x-rays allow your doctor to identify decay in areas that may not be visible in an oral exam. These x-rays are key in maintaining proper oral health and can help your dentist identify dental problems in early stages that the naked eye cannot detect. Identifying problems early can potentially save you money and pain in the long run and is why getting dental x-rays are very important.

Having your dentist take an x-ray yearly will allow them to keep a record of your oral health and will give them a baseline to compare results to. Other problems that can be identified with x-rays include, but are not limited to, decay, bone loss, gum disease, infection, abscesses, cysts, wisdom teeth, and tumors.

Digital x-rays are offered at OC Healthy Smiles, and are considered safer than film x-rays because they have a lower exposure to radiation than film x-rays. The American Society of Radiologic Technologists reports that a set of standard digital x-rays at your dentist is one of the lowest radiation dose tests they have preformed. They report that it is the same amount of radiation that you would experience when aboard a short flight. The protective radiation equipment that dentist uses to shield the rays also lowers your exposure to these rays exponentially.

The timing of when you should have an x-ray done on your teeth is dependent on a couple of key factors. For example, If you are currently seeing your dentist and have a history or risk of gum disease, your dentist may elect to have x-rays taken as often as every 6 months.

It is customary to have an x-ray at your initial exam so your dentist can have a baseline to record any future changes. If you are seeing a new dentist and you had an x-ray taken within 6 months, then those x-rays can be faxed to your new dentist for their view and records. This is one of the only reasons why x-rays may not be taken at an initial exam.

If you are a patient who has great oral hygiene, regularly visits the dentist, and a limited history of dental or gum disease, it is possible that new x-rays are taken every 1-3 years. This will be based on the judgement of the dental team.
No matter where you fall on the spectrum, it is important to have a regular check-up with your dentist to maintain healthy smile.
If you have more questions about x-rays or would like to schedule your next visit, please call OC Healthy Smiles at (714) 422-0596.

What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays? Posted November 13, 2017 in Uncategorized Should I Have Dental X-Rays taken? What Are The Risks Of Dental X-Rays? Dental x-rays allow your doctor to identify decay in areas that may not be visible in an oral exam. These x-rays are key in maintaining proper oral health and can help your dentist identify dental problems in early stages that the naked eye cannot detect. Identifying problems early can potentially save you money and pain in the long run and is why getting dental x-rays are very important. Having your dentist take an x-ray yearly will allow them to keep a record of your oral health and will give them a baseline to compare results to. Other problems that can be identified with x-rays include, but are not limited to, decay, bone loss, gum disease, infection, abscesses, cysts, wisdom teeth, and tumors. I Do Not Want My Loved Ones To Be Exposed To Too Much Radiation. How Do I Lower The Radiation Effect, And Maintain Good Oral Health? Digital x-rays are offered at OC Healthy Smiles, and are considered safer than film x-rays because they have a lower exposure to radiation than film x-rays. The American Society of Radiologic Technologists reports that a set of standard digital x-rays at your dentist is one of the lowest radiation dose tests they have preformed. They report that it is the same amount of radiation that you would experience when aboard a short flight. The protective radiation equipment that dentist uses to shield the rays also lowers your exposure to these rays exponentially. How Often Should I Have an X-Ray of My Teeth? The timing of when you should have an x-ray done on your teeth is dependent on a couple of key factors. For example, If you are currently seeing your dentist and have a history or risk of gum disease, your dentist may elect to have x-rays taken as often as every 6 months. It is customary to have an x-ray at your initial exam so your dentist can have a baseline to record any future changes. If you are seeing a new dentist and you had an x-ray taken within 6 months, then those x-rays can be faxed to your new dentist for their view and records. This is one of the only reasons why x-rays may not be taken at an initial exam. If you are a patient who has great oral hygiene, regularly visits the dentist, and a limited history of dental or gum disease, it is possible that new x-rays are taken every 1-3 years. This will be based on the judgement of the dental team. No matter where you fall on the spectrum, it is important to have a regular check-up with your dentist to maintain healthy smile. If you have more questions about x-rays or would like to schedule your next visit, please call OC Healthy Smiles at (714) 422-0596. pages.metaDescription: Congratulations! You just got engaged and you are going to marry that special someone! When thinking of planning a wedding their are many things that come to mind, but have you thought of giving your dentist a call? Looking your best on your big day is more that just finding the perfect dress, it's a

headings.text: I Just Got Engaged, Now What?

paragraphs: Posted November 15, 2017 in Uncategorized

Congratulations! You just got engaged and you are going to marry that special someone! When thinking of planning a wedding their are many things that come to mind, but have you thought of giving your dentist a call?

Looking your best on your big day is more that just finding the perfect dress, it’s a feeling. When you feel your best, you look your best. Before your schedule becomes packed with cake tastings, venue visits, and honeymoon planning, make sure you make an appointment with your dentist.

You want the perfect smile for your wedding day, and if you start early, you can achieve that! If your teeth are not aligned correctly or just don’t look as good as you want, ask your dentist about Invisalign and Veneers.

Invisalign is the clear alternative to braces and it will straighten your smile without the hassle of metal brackets or brace face. Invisalign uses a series of clear trays to re-align your smile. With new technological advances, Invisalign is now able to correct a majority of cases from mild up to complex with the new SmartTrack technology. If you want you teeth straighten with Invisalign, but only have a few months before your wedding, ask your Dentist if Propel is right for you. Propel safely speeds up your treatment time 50% faster and goes hand-in-hand with Invisalign.

If your teeth are aligned properly, but you don’t like the shape or size of your teeth, veneers can be placed on top of your teeth to give you a more appealing and stunning smile. Veneers can also be used to cover stained teeth that won’t respond to teeth whitening treatment.
A wedding party must, is teeth whitening. Depending on your time limitations, their are a couple different options for whitening.

In-office whitening, using EZ-White technology, will whiten your teeth up to 8 shades lighter at a 1-hour visit. Many patients who come for in-office whitening with EZ-White experience little to no sensitivity during and after the treatment.

Another option you have is custom whitening trays that you take home to use. Custom whitening trays are made by your dentist at a quick visit and can be used for multiple at-home whitening applications. The trays are filled with whitening gel and will give you a whiter smile in 5-7 days.

The final option for teeth whitening is over the counter whitening strips. These strips can be as effective as the take home whitening trays, but since they are not a custom fit, some people find that they have discomfort. This is also not the healthiest long term option, since it can damage your gums.

Taking care of your oral healthy will do more than make your photos better. OC Healthy Smiles is a Premier Provider of Invisalign and our dentists specialize in cosmetic dentistry. Call (714) 422-0596 to learn more about Invisalign, Propel, Veneers, and whitening or to schedule your appointment today!

headings.text: What Do I Do If My Tooth Hurts?
If you can’t get to the dentist immediately, here are some tips on how to relieve the pain until you can be seen
When do I go to the ER?

paragraphs: Posted November 01, 2017 in Uncategorized
Do you have a toothache or just a pain in one of your teeth? Here is a great guide on what to do.
Schedule an exam so they can treat your situation accordingly. Remember, OC Healthy Smiles is open early mornings, evening, and weekends if an emergency arises

1. Floss around the sore tooth to remove bits of food that may be stuck in your teeth 2. Rinse with warm salt water 3. Use a cold compress to reduce inflammation 4. Use Advil or Ibuprofen to further reduce swelling, inflammation, and relieve pain
1. If you have trouble breathing or swallowing 2. If your eyes are swollen or swelling shut

Their are a number of things that could cause a toothache, and our first priority is to get you our of pain and to find out how to proper treat your situation. If you are experiencing a toothache, please call (714) 545-0484 to schedule your appointment.

headings.text: My Temporary Crown Fell Out – What do I do?

paragraphs: Posted November 06, 2017 in Uncategorized

Do you have a temporary crown or cap on and did it come off? Great news is that this can usually be fixed without an additional dental visit. Visit a local pharmacy and purchase dental adhesive to reattach the temporary. Make sure to contact you dentist and inform them that it fell off since they may want you to come in sooner. Try not to eat on the side of your mouth that the temporary is located, this will help prevent the crown from coming off again.
One benefits of coming to OC Healthy Smiles is same-day CEREC crowns. This means that you don’t have to worry about a temporary falling out or an extra dental visit to have a temporary replaced.
Call (714) 422-0596 to schedule your next exam with our highly skilled dentists.

headings.text: Sleep Apnea and Your Orange County, CA, Dentist
How Do I Know If I Have Sleep Apnea?

paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

In our Costa Mesa, CA, dentists’ office, we believe strongly in taking a comprehensive approach to dentistry. After all, maintaining your good health involves a lot more than just your teeth. One major health concern that some people deal with is sleep apnea, and what many don’t realize is that your Orange County, CA, dentist may actually be able to help you with this potentially life-threatening condition!

Sleep apnea is a disorder that affect you while you are sleeping, and it is marked by one or more pauses in breathing throughout the night. When you stop breathing in your sleep, your body and brain begins to be deprived of oxygen. Eventually, your body realizes that a potential emergency is about to happen, so it jump starts you into breathing again – often with a sudden, sharp intake of breath. If you have ever woken up coughing or gasping, that could certainly have been the result of sleep apnea.
If you show any of the following signs or symptoms, then you should speak with your health care provider about being evaluated for sleep apnea.
In addition, your sleep partner, if you have one, may observe one or more of the following while you are asleep:

When your body experiences an oxygen shortage every night for months or even years, it can begin to have all kinds of health problems. Sleep apnea increases your risk for heart disease, diabetes, stroke, weight gain, memory loss, anxiety, and decreased libido. In addition, your sustained exhaustion from not sleeping well at night can affect your overall quality of life and general sense of happiness.

One thing that doctors often prescribe for sleep apnea sufferers is a CPAP machine that forces air into your lungs. Unfortunately, CPAP machines come with their own difficulties. From the loud noise of the air flow to the restrictiveness of the mask and tubes, CPAP can end up causes a whole different type of sleep disturbance for you!

What many doctors don’t tell their patients is that there is a great alternative to CPAP therapy that you can get at many Orange County, CA, dental offices. The snore guards and sleep apnea devices provided by dentists are small, unobtrusive, and every bit as effective at treating sleep apnea as a CPAP machine.

If you have been diagnosed with sleep apnea, or if you believe you may have sleep apnea, then talk to you doctor. Then give your Orange County dentist a call to find out whether they offer sleep apnea solutions. If they don’t, feel free to reach out to OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA, where our sleep apnea treatment can get you back to feeling rested and happy.

This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. Our Costa Mesa, CA, dental office would be pleased to discuss your unique circumstances and needs as they relate to these topics. paragraphs: Posted January 23, 2017 in Uncategorized
By: Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS

Wearing braces has come a long way since the metal train tracks running across the teeth. After all, who wants to feel like a self-conscious teenager again with a mouth full of metal? Treatment costs are about the same, so it is worth exploring if this technique is the right one for you. structuredData.description: By: Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS The true beauty of wearing Invisalign aligners to straighten teeth is that OC Healthy Smile Blog Ad 4 they are practically invisible! pages.bodyText: Skip to main content We're Open! See Our Safety Measures Book Now Call to Schedule Your Appointment - (714) 422-0596 About Dr. Mark Nguyen Dr. Joelle Tavitian Dr. Austin Burnett Dr. Gaby Chung Dr. Jenny Sunwoo OurTeam Our Mission Community Outreach We Can Help You With Invisalign Teeth Whitening Porcelain Veneers Root Canal Teeth Cleaning Dental Bridges Dental Implants Oral Surgery Cavities / Filling Dental Crowns Dental Sealants Dentures Fluoride Treatment Gum Treatment Oral Cancer Screening Tooth Extraction Why OC Healthy Smiles Extensive Experience Guarantees All-In-One Dental Practice Top Invisalign Provider Advanced Technology Consultative Approach Emergency Appointments Flexible Payment Options Photo Gallery Reviews Resources New Patient Information Invisalign Questionnaire Educational Videos Smile Care Options OC Healthy Smiles – Smile Saver Plan Payment Options Make a Payment The Facts About Fillings Cancellation Policy Blog Join Our Team Contact Contact Us Concierge / VIP Refer a Friend Book Now Get in Touch (714) 422-0596 Follow Us Facebook Instagram Youtube Twitter Yelp Free custom whitening trays for all new patients Blog Seven Things People Wish They Knew Before Invisalign Posted January 23, 2017 in Uncategorized By: Dr. Mark Nguyen DDS The true beauty of wearing Invisalign aligners to straighten teeth is that OC Healthy Smile Blog Ad 4 they are practically invisible! Wearing braces has come a long way since the metal train tracks running across the teeth. Yes, those still exist, but Invisalign offers a practically undetectable option for women and men who want to have gorgeous smiles, but don’t necessarily want co-workers to know they are wearing braces. After all, who wants to feel like a self-conscious teenager again with a mouth full of metal? Treatment costs are about the same, so it is worth exploring if this technique is the right one for you. To help you weigh your options, here are seven things people wish they knew before Invisalign. You are in good company Statistics indicate that 74% of adults have crooked or misaligned teeth. Invisalign treatment can be used for a variety of issues, including (but not limited to) protruding teeth, crowding, gaps, spacing, overbite and underbite. How adjustments work An Invisalign-trained dentist creates a treatment plan which includes custom-made aligners that gently move the teeth into place. They are made out of plastic rather than metal. As progress is made, a new set of aligners is introduced every two weeks. Follow-up visits to your dentist every six weeks assure that the treatment is on track. You have to wear them most of the day It is recommended that patients wear the braces for 20-22 hours a day to achieve the best results. The overall length of treatment averages around 12 months and is ultimately based on individual needs. You must remove your aligner to eat The great news is that with Invisalign you don’t have to give up any foods during the treatment process. You can have anything you want. Yes, that includes chocolate! The key is to remove the aligners before you eat or drink, with water being the exception. This prevents staining or damage to the aligners and ultimately keeps them unnoticeable when you are wearing them again. When you are done eating, simply brush the aligners with warm water and wear. There is pressure but no pain One of the key benefits of Invisalign over traditional braces is that they are not painful. The aligners are made of a smooth BPA-free plastic which is non-abrasive to the cheek and gums. There is a little pressure each time new aligners are introduced, which is a sign that the treatment is working. You have to brush twice Remove the aligners and then brush and floss as you normally would. However, the new thing you’ll want to add to the beauty regimen is taking a toothbrush and toothpaste along to work. After eating, simply brush away food and beverage residue prior to putting the Invisalign back in your mouth. Cleaning the aligners is as simple as using a toothbrush and warm water. Invisalign also has a cleaning solution available. They should be cleaned every time after eating. Better health Making the investment to straighten your teeth will produce big health benefits in the end. Properly aligned teeth enable better daily oral hygiene, reducing the incidence of tooth decay, gum disease and more serious issues such as heart disease, diabetes, pneumonia, and more. headings.text: Don’t Let Gum Disease Affect Your Healthy Smile
Gingivitis: The Beginning Signs of Gum Disease

paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

The majority of tooth loss experienced by Americans today is the direct result of undiagnosed or untreated gum disease. This is particularly unfortunate, because gum disease is a completely preventable condition! Our top-rated dentists at OC Healthy Smiles in Costa Mesa, CA, are here to educate you on the causes and signs of gum disease, aka periodontal disease. With a little extra care, you can keep your teeth and gums healthy and intact.

Gingivitis, the early stage of periodontal disease, is your body’s natural response to being attacked by the bacteria contained in plaque. Gingivitis is usually identified through the following symptoms:

Sometimes, gingivitis can be present without you even realize it. As long as you see your dentist in Orange County, CA, for a dental cleaning and exam once every six months, your dentist will be able to identify whether you have gingivitis or not.

Gingivitis can be exacerbated if you don’t regularly brush and floss, if you use tobacco or cigarettes, and if you aren’t eating a proper, nutritional diet. Gingivitis can also be made more likely by genetics or hormone changes – in fact, many pregnant women find themselves suffering from gingivitis because of the influx of pregnancy hormones!

Like most medical issues, gum disease won’t go away all by itself. If you and your dentist don’t work together to address your gingivitis, it will eventually transform into advanced gum disease, also called periodontal disease. At this stage of gum disease, your gums will be quite sore, swollen, and bloody. That’s not the end of it, either!

Because gum disease attacks your gums and underlying bone, your teeth lose their support structure. As the periodontal disease progresses, your teeth will start to loosen and wiggle, and eventually they will fall out. Missing teeth means more than having gaps in your smile, too! When you lose teeth, your ability to bite and chew is compromised, and you can even begin to have difficulty with speech articulation.

Gum disease doesn’t stop at your mouth, either. Diabetes, respiratory disease, heart disease, and even some forms of cancer have been linked to the same bacteria that causes gum disease. By stopping your gum disease while it is still in the gingivitis stage, you are keeping these bacteria from entering your blood stream and potential saving yourself from far worse health problems down the road.
In short, gum disease is definitely an issue you want to avoid, so make sure you work with your Orange County, CA, dentist to ensure you are giving your oral health the attention it deserves.

This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. Our Costa Mesa, CA, dental office would be pleased to discuss your unique circumstances and needs as they relate to these topics. At this stage of gum disease, your gums will be quite sore, swollen, and bloody. That’s not the end of it, either! Because gum disease attacks your gums and underlying bone, your teeth lose their support structure. As the periodontal disease progresses, your teeth will start to loosen and wiggle, and eventually they will fall out. Missing teeth means more than having gaps in your smile, too! When you lose teeth, your ability to bite and chew is compromised, and you can even begin to have difficulty with speech articulation. Gum Disease Knows No Boundaries Gum disease doesn’t stop at your mouth, either. Diabetes, respiratory disease, heart disease, and even some forms of cancer have been linked to the same bacteria that causes gum disease. By stopping your gum disease while it is still in the gingivitis stage, you are keeping these bacteria from entering your blood stream and potential saving yourself from far worse health problems down the road. In short, gum disease is definitely an issue you want to avoid, so make sure you work with your Orange County, CA, dentist to ensure you are giving your oral health the attention it deserves. This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. pages.title: Your Healthy Mouth – The Importance of Oral Health Care at Home - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: You trust the health of your teeth and gums to your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, but who’s in charge of keeping your mouth healthy in between your 6-month dental checkups? YOU, that’s who! Our OC Healthy Smiles dentists are here to give you a few tips on how to make sure your teeth stay in

headings.text: Your Healthy Mouth – The Importance of Oral Health Care at Home
Tip #4: Ask Your Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist about Sealants

paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

You trust the health of your teeth and gums to your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, but who’s in charge of keeping your mouth healthy in between your 6-month dental checkups? YOU, that’s who! Our OC Healthy Smiles dentists are here to give you a few tips on how to make sure your teeth stay in tip-top shape during the months between your regular dental cleanings in Orange County, CA.

When you indulge in sugar-heavy treats such as candy or soda, the sugary residue left behind on your teeth creates a breeding ground for unhealthy bacteria. Given enough time, that bacteria can result in a variety of problems, including tooth decay, cavities, and gum disease. To avoid getting “sugar bugs” in your mouth, limit your intake of high-sugar and high-corn-syrup foods and drinks. If you do have a sugary treat, then brush your teeth afterward – or at least rinse your mouth with plain water to dilute the sugar.

You’ve probably heard this one before, but it certainly bears repeating. Brushing at least twice a day and flossing at least once a day not only removes the sugar and food particles that attract bacteria; it also removes plaque before it can harden into tartar. Tartar can form in under 24 hours, so missing even one day of brushing means you will likely end up with tartar that your tooth brush won’t be able to remove.

Fluoride is a mineral that can actually repair very early damage to your teeth on a microscopic level. By drinking fluoride-infused water (such as city tap water) and by rinsing your mouth once a day with a fluoride-based mouthwash, you can help to keep your teeth strong and less prone to chipping or cracks.

Dental sealants are a preventative material that your Orange County, CA, dentist can put over the deep crevices in your back teeth. This keeps your teeth from having too many crevices where food particles or bacteria can get caught. This way, dental sealants from your Orange County dentist can stop cavities or tooth decay before it even has a chance to start. At your next appointment with your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist, ask if sealants would be a good option for you.

Sometimes, cavities just happen, no matter what kind of preventative measures you take for your oral health. However, with these four simple steps, you can make tooth decay and gum disease much less likely to occur in between your regular dental exam and dental cleaning with your Orange County, CA, dentist. Here’s to keeping your mouth happy and healthy!

This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. pages.title: You Lost A Tooth Filling – Don’t Panic! By Your Top-Rated Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist - OC Healthy Smiles
pages.metaDescription: If a tooth filling falls out, it leaves behind an open hole in your tooth that can be both uncomfortable and nerve wracking. You don't need to panic, though, because losing a dental filling is actually one of the more common dental emergencies. That means your dentist should be able to get you fixed up

headings.text: You Lost A Tooth Filling – Don’t Panic! By Your Top-Rated Costa Mesa, CA, Dentist
What Can Make A Tooth Filling Fall Out?
My Filling Fell Out! What Do I Do?

paragraphs: Posted January 09, 2017 in Uncategorized

If a tooth filling falls out, it leaves behind an open hole in your tooth that can be both uncomfortable and nerve wracking. You don’t need to panic, though, because losing a dental filling is actually one of the more common dental emergencies. That means your dentist should be able to get you fixed up and back to your regular routine in no time.

A tooth filling is exactly what the name sounds like: a special material that fills in a hole in your tooth. When you have tooth decay, your Orange County, CA, dentist cleans out the damaged part of your tooth before it can spread to the other, healthy parts of your tooth. Then your dentist fills the spot where the decay used to be with your dental filling. There are a range of options for filling material, including gold, porcelain, the old-school silver amalgam (made up of mercury mixed with other materials) that so many of us have in the backs of our mouths, and the increasingly popular tooth-colored composite resin.

The material that makes up a tooth filling is strong, but that doesn’t mean it lasts forever. Dental fillings can get worn down from all the chewing you do over the years, and so they eventually need to be replaced. Usually, your Orange County, CA, dentist will identify worn fillings during your regular dental cleaning and exam, so the filling gets replaced before it gets a chance to fall out. Nevertheless, there are a variety of factors that can cause fillings to fall out unexpectedly, for example:

As we’ve already said, don’t panic! Wash your hands, and then carefully check your mouth to remove the missing filling, as well as any pieces of broken tooth that may be there. Then call your Costa Mesa, CA, dental office right away. Tell the receptionist that you’ve lost a filling and need an emergency appointment. Most dentists keep emergency time slots available in their schedules for cases like this, because they know that you shouldn’t leave the sensitive underlying area of your tooth exposed. It’s important that you get your missing filling taken care of by a professional as quickly as possible.
If you have to wait before your dentist can see you, then there are a few steps you can take to avoid infection and reduce your discomfort.

Lost dental fillings happen all the time. Unfortunately for some of us, they can happen at the least opportune moments. But there’s no need to panic. Just keep the area clean and, most importantly, see your dentist as soon as possible to prevent infection and discomfort. If your Orange County, CA, dentist can’t provide you with a timely emergency appointment, or if you don’t currently have a dentist, then OC Healthy Smiles will be happy to fix your lost filling and get your smile back.

This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. paragraphs: Posted January 10, 2017 in Uncategorized

Any of these types of problems can be addressed with a smile makeover from your local Orange County, CA, dental office. For example, at our OC Healthy Smiles office in Costa Mesa, CA, we offer a wide variety of smile solutions, such as teeth whitening, dental bonding, dental implants, and dentures. Here is a crash course in what each of these procedures is and what it can do for your loved one’s smile.

Unlike with over-the-counter teeth whitening kits, a professional teeth whitening treatment from your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist can get a smile brighter in less time – AND with fewer risks! When not performed properly, a teeth whitening treatment can result in weakened tooth enamel, permanent tooth sensitivity, gum irritation, or other problems. When overseen by your dentist, however, you can be sure that the chemical concentration of the whitener is safe for your individual needs.

If your valentine’s smile has gotten dimmer over the years because of coffee, cigarettes, or other types of stains, then scheduling them an appointment with your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist for tooth whitening may be the perfect gift this Valentine’s Day!

Bonding is a cost-effective cosmetic solution with which your Orange County dentist can correct small blemishes on your teeth, such as misshapen teeth, shortened teeth, gaps between teeth, and tooth stains that won’t respond to teeth whitening. With this procedure, a durable, tooth-colored material is bonded directly to your tooth to restore it to a more pristine condition.
Dental bonding is painless and fast to perform. It involves no invasive treatment, and it can bring about incredible changes to your or your loved one’s smile.

Losing a tooth or teeth can cause you to feel embarrassed about your smile – not to mention the chewing, biting, and even speaking difficulties that some people suffer because of missing teeth! If you or your valentine hesitate to smile because of missing teeth, then getting dentures or dental implants from your Orange County, CA, dentist may be the perfect life-changing gift this Valentine’s Day!

Both dentures and implants are artificial teeth meant to replace your missing ones. In both cases, the artificial teeth look natural and help to restore chewing and speaking function that you may have lost when you lost your teeth. The main difference between dentures and dental implants from your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist is in their permanence. While dentures are typically removable, dental implants are – as the name suggests – permanently implanted into your mouth with artificial tooth roots.
Your Costa Mesa, CA, dentist can discuss the benefits of dentures and dental implants with you or your loved one to decide which would be the best option for your unique lifestyle and needs.

This information is not meant as medical advice. It is provided solely for education. Our Costa Mesa, CA, dental office would be pleased to discuss your unique circumstances and needs as they relate to these topics.
